column1: When you travel__________the other side of the world, jetlag is a real problem.
You find yourself awake____________the middle of the night and you feel like going to bed____________the morning, just when everyone around you is getting up.
Jetlag happens when you go___________a country where the time is very different.
For example, you might leave London___________midday and fly____________Los Angeles.
The flight takes about eleven hours, so when you arrive__________Los Angeles airport, your body thinks you're there____________11 pm.
But Los Angeles is eight hours behind London, so you actually get there___________3 pm local time.
So,___________midnight Los Angeles time, your body (which still thinks it's___________London) says it's 8 am. It takes a few days for your body clock to change.
